findings.issue: InvitationCompletePage displays unvalidated `error` query param. The `error` URL query parameter (line 20) is read via `qp.get('error')` and displayed directly to the user at line 28 (`setError(\`OAuth error: ${oauthError}\`)`) and rendered at line 155. An attacker can craft a URL like `/invitations/x/complete?error=Please+re-authenticate+at+https://evil.com` that displays a phishing message on a legitimate domain. React prevents XSS but not social engineering. Same vulnerability class as the LoginPage known gap but on a separate page/code path not covered by the existing known gap.
findings.remediation: Validate oauthError against a whitelist of known OAuth error codes (access_denied, invalid_request, server_error, etc.) and display a generic message for unknown values, or strip any URL-like patterns from the error string before display.

findings.issue: OAuth invitation flow catch blocks clear existing valid session on transient API errors. In InvitationCompletePage, if `redeemOAuth` succeeds but `acceptInvitation` fails due to a transient error (network timeout, 500), the catch block at line 90 runs `localStorage.removeItem('access_token')`. Since the new token from `redeemOAuth` was never stored (line 77 only executes on success), this removes the user's ORIGINAL session token. Same pattern exists in InvitationPage.tsx:60 and AppRouter.tsx:162. A user with a valid session who clicks an invitation link and encounters a transient server error loses their existing session and must re-authenticate.
findings.remediation: Only clear access_token on auth-specific errors (401/403 ApiError). For other errors, clear only verifier and invitation token. Alternatively, snapshot the pre-existing token before the flow and restore it on non-auth failures.
